nodes.context: White supremacist prison and street gang operating primarily in Texas and federal prisons, involved in drug trafficking, murder, and other violent crimes.
Notorious prison gang and crime syndicate founded in California in the 1960s, with branches in federal and state prisons.
White supremacist prison gang formed in the 1980s by former members of the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas, rival to ABT.
Black prison gang that fought a war with the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas and was nearly exterminated.
Prison gang that obtained a peace treaty with the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas in the early 1990s.
Prison gang that was 'parked' (no longer allowed to recruit) after wars with the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas in 1995/1996.
White supremacist gang targeted in a 2018 federal crackdown alongside other groups.
White supremacist gang targeted in a 2018 federal crackdown.
White supremacist gang targeted in a 2018 federal crackdown.
Mexican drug cartel listed as an ally of the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas in the infobox.

edges.context: Despite similar names, the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas is not affiliated with the Aryan Brotherhood; the AB denied their request to start a chapter.
The ABT and AC have been in multiple wars: 1986-1994, 1998-2009, and ongoing since 2015.
ABT fought a war with the Mandingo Warriors, nearly exterminating them within the prison system.
The ABT and Mexican Mafia came away with a peace treaty in the early 1990s.
ABT fought wars with Texas Mafia in 1995/1996, resulting in TM being 'parked' (no longer allowed to recruit).
The Gulf Cartel is listed as an ally of the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas in the infobox.
